articles.headline: Harvard Found the Missing Link Between Your Gut and Your Mood
articles.body: A new Harvard Medical School study may explain why gut health and depression are so tightly connected. Researchers found that a gut bacterium called Morganella morganii, when it interacts with a common environmental pollutant, produces a molecule that triggers inflammation — something strongly tied to depression. The finding, published in the Journal of the American Chemical Society, points to the immune system — not just the brain — as a potential treatment target. It also opens a window into how other gut microbes may quietly shape your mental health.
articles.takeaway: A diverse, well-fed gut microbiome matters for your mood, not just your digestion. Fiber, fermented foods, and less processed junk are still your best levers.

articles.topic: cortisol excess resistant hypertension MOMENTUM study
articles.headline: Why Your Blood Pressure Meds Might Not Be Working (Hint: It's a Hormone)
articles.body: A major new study called MOMENTUM — the largest of its kind in the US — found that 27 percent of patients with resistant high blood pressure actually have too much cortisol, not just a plumbing problem. Researchers at Mount Sinai analyzed over 1,000 patients across 50 centers and found hypercortisolism is far more common in these patients than doctors previously thought. Resistant hypertension is when blood pressure stays high even on three or more medications. Excess cortisol is also linked to weight gain, muscle loss, and blood sugar issues.
articles.takeaway: If your blood pressure won't budge despite medication, ask your doctor whether a cortisol test makes sense. Chronic stress and poor sleep are two modifiable drivers of elevated cortisol.

articles.topic: creatine single dose cognitive performance sleep deprivation
articles.headline: Creatine Helped Sleep-Deprived Brains Think Clearer in a New Trial
articles.body: A new study published in Nutrients found that a single dose of creatine monohydrate improved cognitive performance by 12 percent during 21 hours of sleep deprivation. Researchers found the biggest gains in logical reasoning and language-related processing speed. The science makes sense — your brain stores a small amount of creatine and relies on it for energy, especially when sleep-deprived cells are under metabolic stress. The effect appears dose-dependent, with higher doses showing stronger results on memory tasks.
articles.takeaway: Already taking creatine for the gym? It may also be quietly protecting your focus on short-sleep days. The daily 3-5g maintenance dose is where most people should start.

product.name: Thorne Creatine Monohydrate
product.blurb: The most researched supplement in history. Works for strength, brain health, and endurance.

articles.headline: 83% of People Say Something Is Getting in the Way of Good Sleep
articles.body: ResMed's 2026 Global Sleep Survey of 30,000 people across 13 countries found that 83 percent of respondents face barriers to quality sleep. Women are harder hit — 56 percent say they only get a good night's sleep four days a week or less. Nearly 70 percent have called in sick to work at least once because they were too tired after a bad night. The silver lining: wearable sleep trackers are surging, with nearly 4 in 10 people now checking their sleep data at least weekly.
articles.takeaway: Tracking your sleep is a start, but the data only helps if you act on it. Pick one thing — consistent bedtime, darker room, phone out of reach — and protect it like a meeting.

product.name: Manta Sleep Mask
product.blurb: Total darkness is one of the highest-leverage sleep improvements you can make.

quote.text: You must do the thing you think you cannot do.
quote.author: Eleanor Roosevelt
quote.context: First Lady, diplomat, and one of the most influential human rights advocates of the 20th century.

workout.title: Dead Hang and Row Power Circuit
workout.warmup: Spend 90 seconds doing arm circles, shoulder rolls, and slow cat-cow stretches on all fours. Then hang from the bar for 20-30 seconds with a relaxed grip to decompress the spine and wake up your lats before loading them.

exercises.name: Dead Hang
exercises.notes: Grip the bar with palms facing away, shoulders engaged — not shrugging into your ears. Work up to 45 seconds. Great for grip and shoulder health.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Row
exercises.notes: Anchor band at chest height, step back for tension. Drive elbows behind you and squeeze shoulder blades together at the top. Control the return — do not let the band snap you forward.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Inverted Table Row (using a low bar or desk edge)
exercises.notes: Lie under a sturdy surface, grip the edge, and pull your chest to it. Keep your body rigid like a plank. Too easy? Elevate your feet.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
exercises.notes: Hold a light resistance band at chest height with both hands shoulder-width apart. Pull it apart until your arms form a T, squeezing your rear delts. Slow and controlled.

exercises.name: Hollow Body Hold
exercises.notes: Lie on your back, press your lower back into the floor, raise shoulders and legs slightly. This is a core anchor movement — essential for strong, stable pulling. Bend knees if needed to maintain lower back contact.

workout.cooldown: Hang passively from the bar for 20 seconds to decompress, then do a doorframe chest stretch — place both forearms on a door frame and lean through gently for 30 seconds to open the front body after all that pulling.

product.name: Gymnastic Rings - Double Circle Wood
product.blurb: Chris trains on rings 2x per week. The most effective upper body tool that fits in a bag.

meal.title: Lemon Herb Chicken and Quinoa Bowl
ingredients: Salt, pepper, garlic powder to taste
meal.instructions: 1. Cook quinoa in chicken broth per package directions (about 15 min). 2. Season chicken with lemon juice, oregano, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Cook in a skillet over medium-high heat with olive oil, 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through. Slice. 3. Build your bowl: quinoa base, then spinach, tomatoes, and cucumber. Top with sliced chicken. 4. Add a dollop of hummus and a squeeze of lemon to finish.
meal.tip: Grill or bake a double batch of chicken on Sunday and you have lunch sorted for two days — just keep the quinoa and veggies separate until serving so nothing gets soggy.

product.name: Greater Goods Digital Food Scale
product.blurb: Measure your food for two weeks and you will understand your body better than any diet book.
